NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. Set to “The Lilies.” A Contemplation of the sons of Korah. A Song of Love. My heart is overflowing with a good theme; I recite my composition concerning the King; My tongue is the pen of a ready writer.
NKJV.2: You are fairer than the sons of men;  Grace is poured upon Your lips; Therefore God has blessed You forever.
NKJV.3: Gird Your sword upon Your thigh, O Mighty One, With Your glory and Your majesty.
NKJV.4: And in Your majesty ride prosperously because of truth, humility, and righteousness; And Your right hand shall teach You awesome things.
NKJV.5: Your arrows are sharp in the heart of the King’s enemies; The peoples fall under You.
NKJV.6: Your throne, O God, is forever and ever; A scepter of righteousness is the scepter of Your kingdom.
NKJV.7: You love righteousness and hate wickedness; Therefore God, Your God, has anointed You With the oil of gladness more than Your companions.
NKJV.8: All Your garments are scented with myrrh and aloes and cassia, Out of the ivory palaces, by which they have made You glad.
NKJV.9: Kings’ daughters are among Your honorable women; At Your right hand stands the queen in gold from Ophir.
NKJV.10: Listen, O daughter, Consider and incline your ear;  Forget your own people also, and your father’s house;
NKJV.11: So the King will greatly desire your beauty; Because He is your Lord, worship Him.
NKJV.12: And the daughter of Tyre will come with a gift; The rich among the people will seek your favor.
NKJV.13: The royal daughter is all glorious within the palace; Her clothing is woven with gold.
NKJV.14: She shall be brought to the King in robes of many colors; The virgins, her companions who follow her, shall be brought to You.
NKJV.15: With gladness and rejoicing they shall be brought; They shall enter the King’s palace.
NKJV.16: Instead of Your fathers shall be Your sons,  Whom You shall make princes in all the earth.
NKJV.17: I will make Your name to be remembered in all generations; Therefore the people shall praise You forever and ever.

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S> upon Shoshannim<S>7799</S>, for the sons<S>1121</S> of Korah<S>7141</S>, Maschil<S>4905</S>, A Song<S>7892</S> of loves<S>3039</S>. ESV.1: My heart overflows with a pleasing theme; I address my verses to the king; my tongue is like the pen of a ready scribe.
ESV.2: You are the most handsome of the sons of men; grace is poured upon your lips; therefore God has blessed you forever.
ESV.3: Gird your sword on your thigh, O mighty one, in your splendor and majesty!
ESV.4: In your majesty ride out victoriously for the cause of truth and meekness and righteousness; let your right hand teach you awesome deeds!
ESV.5: Your arrows are sharp in the heart of the king's enemies; the peoples fall under you.
ESV.6: Your throne, O God, is forever and ever. The scepter of your kingdom is a scepter of uprightness;
ESV.7: you have loved righteousness and hated wickedness. Therefore God, your God, has anointed you with the oil of gladness beyond your companions;
ESV.8: your robes are all fragrant with myrrh and aloes and cassia. From ivory palaces stringed instruments make you glad;
ESV.9: daughters of kings are among your ladies of honor; at your right hand stands the queen in gold of Ophir.
ESV.10: Hear, O daughter, and consider, and incline your ear: forget your people and your father's house,
ESV.11: and the king will desire your beauty. Since he is your lord, bow to him.
ESV.12: The people  of Tyre will seek your favor with gifts, the richest of the people.
ESV.13: All glorious is the princess in her chamber, with robes interwoven with gold.
ESV.14: In many-colored robes she is led to the king, with her virgin companions following behind her.
ESV.15: With joy and gladness they are led along as they enter the palace of the king.
ESV.16: In place of your fathers shall be your sons; you will make them princes in all the earth.
ESV.17: I will cause your name to be remembered in all generations; therefore nations will praise you forever and ever.

NASB.1: For the choir director; according to the Shoshannim. A Maskil of the sons of Korah. A Song of Love. My heart overflows with a good theme; I address my verses to the King; My tongue is the pen of a ready writer.
NASB.2: You are fairer than the sons of men; Grace is poured upon Your lips; Therefore God has blessed You forever.
NASB.3: Gird Your sword on [Your] thigh, O Mighty One, [In] Your splendor and Your majesty!
NASB.4: And in Your majesty ride on victoriously, For the cause of truth and meekness [and] righteousness; Let Your right hand teach You awesome things.
NASB.5: Your arrows are sharp; The peoples fall under You; [Your arrows are] in the heart of the King's enemies.
NASB.6: Your throne, O God, is forever and ever; A scepter of uprightness is the scepter of Your kingdom.
NASB.7: You have loved righteousness and hated wickedness; Therefore God, Your God, has anointed You With the oil of joy above Your fellows.
NASB.8: All Your garments are [fragrant with] myrrh and aloes [and] cassia; Out of ivory palaces stringed instruments have made You glad.
NASB.9: Kings' daughters are among Your noble ladies; At Your right hand stands the queen in gold from Ophir.
NASB.10: Listen, O daughter, give attention and incline your ear: Forget your people and your father's house;
NASB.11: Then the King will desire your beauty. Because He is your Lord, bow down to Him.
NASB.12: The daughter of Tyre [will come] with a gift; The rich among the people will seek your favor.
NASB.13: The King's daughter is all glorious within; Her clothing is interwoven with gold.
NASB.14: She will be led to the King in embroidered work; The virgins, her companions who follow her, Will be brought to You.
NASB.15: They will be led forth with gladness and rejoicing; They will enter into the King's palace.
NASB.16: In place of your fathers will be your sons; You shall make them princes in all the earth.
NASB.17: I will cause Your name to be remembered in all generations; Therefore the peoples will give You thanks forever and ever.

NLT.1:
Beautiful words stir my heart.
I will recite a lovely poem about the king,
for my tongue is like the pen of a skillful poet.
NLT.2:
You are the most handsome of all.
Gracious words stream from your lips.
God himself has blessed you forever.
NLT.3:
Put on your sword, O mighty warrior!
You are so glorious, so majestic!
NLT.4:
In your majesty, ride out to victory,
defending truth, humility, and justice.
Go forth to perform awe-inspiring deeds!
NLT.5:
Your arrows are sharp, piercing your enemies’ hearts.
The nations fall beneath your feet.
NLT.6:
Your throne, O God, endures forever and ever.
You rule with a scepter of justice.
NLT.7:
You love justice and hate evil.
Therefore God, your God, has anointed you,
pouring out the oil of joy on you more than on anyone else.
NLT.8:
Myrrh, aloes, and cassia perfume your robes.
In ivory palaces the music of strings entertains you.
NLT.9:
Kings’ daughters are among your noble women.
At your right side stands the queen,
wearing jewelry of finest gold from Ophir!
NLT.10:
Listen to me, O royal daughter; take to heart what I say.
Forget your people and your family far away.
NLT.11:
For your royal husband delights in your beauty;
honor him, for he is your lord.
NLT.12:
The princess of Tyre will shower you with gifts.
The wealthy will beg your favor.
NLT.13:
The bride, a princess, looks glorious
in her golden gown.
NLT.14:
In her beautiful robes, she is led to the king,
accompanied by her bridesmaids.
NLT.15:
What a joyful and enthusiastic procession
as they enter the king’s palace!
NLT.16:
Your sons will become kings like their father.
You will make them rulers over many lands.
NLT.17:
I will bring honor to your name in every generation.
Therefore, the nations will praise you forever and ever.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. Set to “The Lilies.” A contemplation by the sons of Korah. A wedding song. My heart overflows with a noble theme. I recite my verses for the king. My tongue is like the pen of a skillful writer.
WEB.2: You are the most excellent of the sons of men. Grace has anointed your lips, therefore God has blessed you forever.
WEB.3: Strap your sword on your thigh, mighty one: your splendor and your majesty.
WEB.4: In your majesty ride on victoriously on behalf of truth, humility, and righteousness. Let your right hand display awesome deeds.
WEB.5: Your arrows are sharp. The nations fall under you, with arrows in the heart of the king’s enemies.
WEB.6: Your throne, God, is forever and ever. A scepter of equity is the scepter of your kingdom.
WEB.7: You have loved righteousness, and hated wickedness. Therefore God, your God, has anointed you with the oil of gladness above your fellows.
WEB.8: All your garments smell like myrrh, aloes, and cassia. Out of ivory palaces stringed instruments have made you glad.
WEB.9: Kings’ daughters are among your honorable women. At your right hand the queen stands in gold of Ophir.
WEB.10: Listen, daughter, consider, and turn your ear. Forget your own people, and also your father’s house.
WEB.11: So the king will desire your beauty, honor him, for he is your lord.
WEB.12: The daughter of Tyre comes with a gift. The rich among the people entreat your favor.
WEB.13: The princess inside is all glorious. Her clothing is interwoven with gold.
WEB.14: She shall be led to the king in embroidered work. The virgins, her companions who follow her, shall be brought to you.
WEB.15: With gladness and rejoicing they shall be led. They shall enter into the king’s palace.
WEB.16: Your sons will take the place of your fathers. You shall make them princes in all the earth.
WEB.17: I will make your name to be remembered in all generations. Therefore the peoples shall give you thanks forever and ever.

CSB17.1:
For the choir director: according to “The Lilies.” ⓔ A of the sons of Korah. A love song. [1] My heart is moved by a noble theme
as I recite my verses to the king;
my tongue is the pen of a skillful writer. ⓕ
CSB17.2:
You are the most handsome of men; [1]
grace flows from your lips. ⓖ
Therefore God has blessed you forever. ⓗ
CSB17.3:
Mighty warrior, strap your sword at your side.
In your majesty and splendor ⓘ  —
CSB17.4:
in your splendor ride triumphantly
in the cause of truth, humility, and justice.
May your right hand show your awe-inspiring acts. ⓙ
CSB17.5:
Your sharpened arrows pierce the hearts of the king’s enemies;
the peoples fall under you. ⓚ
CSB17.6:
Your throne, ⓛ God, is [2] forever and ever;
the scepter of your kingdom is a scepter of justice. ⓜ
CSB17.7:
You love righteousness and hate wickedness; ⓝ
therefore God, your God, has anointed you with the oil of joy
more than your companions.
CSB17.8:
Myrrh, aloes, and cassia perfume all your garments;
from ivory palaces harps bring you joy. ⓐ
CSB17.9:
Kings’ daughters are among your honored women; ⓑ
the queen, adorned with gold from Ophir,
stands at your right hand. ⓒ
CSB17.10:
Listen, daughter, pay attention and consider:
forget your people and your father’s house, ⓓ
CSB17.11:
and the king will desire your beauty.
Bow down to him, for he is your lord. ⓔ
CSB17.12:
The daughter of Tyre, the wealthy people,
will seek your favor with gifts. ⓕ
CSB17.13:
In her chamber, the royal daughter is all glorious,
her clothing embroidered with gold. ⓖ
CSB17.14:
In colorful garments she is led to the king;
after her, the virgins, her companions, are brought to you. ⓗ
CSB17.15:
They are led in with gladness and rejoicing;
they enter the king’s palace. ⓘ
CSB17.16:
Your sons will succeed your ancestors;
you will make them princes throughout the land. ⓙ
CSB17.17:
I will cause your name to be remembered for all generations;
therefore the peoples will praise you forever and ever. ⓚ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician; set to the [tune of] "Lilies." A Psalm of the sons of Korah. A skillful song, or a didactic or reflective poem. A Song of Love. My heart overflows with a good theme;
I address my psalm to the King.
My tongue is like the pen of a skillful writer.
AMP.2:
You are fairer than the sons of men;
Graciousness is poured upon Your lips;
Therefore God has blessed You forever.
AMP.3:
Strap Your sword on Your thigh, O mighty One,
In Your splendor and Your majesty!
AMP.4:
And in Your majesty ride on triumphantly
For the cause of truth and humility and righteousness;
Let Your right hand guide You to awesome things.
AMP.5:
Your arrows are sharp;
The peoples (nations) fall under You;
Your arrows pierce the hearts of the King's enemies.
AMP.6:
Your throne, O God, is forever and ever;
The scepter of uprightness is the scepter of Your kingdom.
AMP.7:
You have loved righteousness (virtue, morality, justice) and hated wickedness;
Therefore God, your God, has anointed You
Above Your companions with the oil of jubilation.
AMP.8:
All Your garments are fragrant with myrrh, aloes and cassia;
From ivory palaces stringed instruments have made You glad.
AMP.9:
Kings' daughters are among Your noble ladies;
At Your right hand stands the queen in gold from Ophir.
AMP.10:
Hear, O daughter, consider and incline your ear [to my instruction]:
Forget your people and your father's house;
AMP.11:
Then the King will desire your beauty;
Because He is your Lord, bow down and honor Him.
AMP.12:
The daughter of Tyre will come with a gift;
The rich among the people will seek your favor.
AMP.13:
Glorious is the King's daughter within [the palace];
Her robe is interwoven with gold.
AMP.14:
She will be brought to the King in embroidered garments;
The virgins, her companions who follow her,
Will be brought to You.
AMP.15:
With gladness and rejoicing will they be led;
They will enter into the King's palace.
AMP.16:
In place of your fathers will be your sons;
You shall make princes in all the land.
AMP.17:
I will make Your name to be remembered in all generations;
Therefore the peoples will praise and give You thanks forever and ever.

MSG.1:
A wedding song of the sons of Korah
My heart bursts its banks,
spilling beauty and goodness.
I pour it out in a poem to the king,
shaping the river into words:
MSG.2:
"You're the handsomest of men;
every word from your lips is sheer grace,
and God has blessed you, blessed you so much.
MSG.3:
Strap your sword to your side, warrior!
Accept praise! Accept due honor!
Ride majestically! Ride triumphantly!
MSG.4:
Ride on the side of truth!
Ride for the righteous meek!
"Your instructions are glow-in-the-dark;
MSG.5:
you shoot sharp arrows
Into enemy hearts; the king's
foes lie down in the dust, beaten.
MSG.6:
"Your throne is God's throne,
ever and always;
The scepter of your royal rule
measures right living.
MSG.7:
You love the right
and hate the wrong.
And that is why God, your very own God,
poured fragrant oil on your head,
Marking you out as king
from among your dear companions.
MSG.8:
"Your ozone-drenched garments
are fragrant with mountain breeze.
Chamber music—from the throne room—
makes you want to dance.
MSG.9:
Kings' daughters are maids in your court,
the Bride glittering with golden jewelry.
MSG.10:
"Now listen, daughter, don't miss a word:
forget your country, put your home behind you.
MSG.11:
Be here—the king is wild for you.
Since he's your lord, adore him.
MSG.12:
Wedding gifts pour in from Tyre;
rich guests shower you with presents."
MSG.13:
(Her wedding dress is dazzling,
lined with gold by the weavers;
MSG.14:
All her dresses and robes
are woven with gold.
She is led to the king,
followed by her virgin companions.
MSG.15:
A procession of joy and laughter!
a grand entrance to the king's palace!)
MSG.16:
"Set your mind now on sons—
don't dote on father and grandfather.
You'll set your sons up as princes
all over the earth.
MSG.17:
I'll make you famous for generations;
you'll be the talk of the town
for a long, long time."

NIV.1:
Psalm 45
For the director of music. To the tune of “Lilies.” Of the Sons of Korah. A maskil. A wedding song.
My heart is stirred by a noble theme
as I recite my verses for the king;
my tongue is the pen of a skillful writer.
NIV.2:
You are the most excellent of men
and your lips have been anointed with grace,
since God has blessed you forever.
NIV.3:
Gird your sword upon your side, O mighty one;
clothe yourself with splendor and majesty.
NIV.4:
In your majesty ride forth victoriously
in behalf of truth, humility and righteousness;
let your right hand display awesome deeds.
NIV.5:
Let your sharp arrows pierce the hearts of the king’s enemies;
let the nations fall beneath your feet.
NIV.6:
Your throne, O God, will last for ever and ever;
a scepter of justice will be the scepter of your kingdom.
NIV.7:
You love righteousness and hate wickedness;
therefore God, your God, has set you above your companions
by anointing you with the oil of joy.
NIV.8:
All your robes are fragrant with myrrh and aloes and cassia;
from palaces adorned with ivory
the music of the strings makes you glad.
NIV.9:
Daughters of kings are among your honored women;
at your right hand is the royal bride in gold of Ophir.
NIV.10:
Listen, O daughter, consider and give ear:
Forget your people and your father’s house.
NIV.11:
The king is enthralled by your beauty;
honor him, for he is your lord.
NIV.12:
The Daughter of Tyre will come with a gift,
men of wealth will seek your favor.
NIV.13:
All glorious is the princess within her chamber;
her gown is interwoven with gold.
NIV.14:
In embroidered garments she is led to the king;
her virgin companions follow her
and are brought to you.
NIV.15:
They are led in with joy and gladness;
they enter the palace of the king.
NIV.16:
Your sons will take the place of your fathers;
you will make them princes throughout the land.
NIV.17:
I will perpetuate your memory through all generations;
therefore the nations will praise you for ever and ever.

NRSVCE.1:
My heart overflows with a goodly theme;
I address my verses to the king;
my tongue is like the pen of a ready scribe.
NRSVCE.2:
You are the most handsome of men;
grace is poured upon your lips;
therefore God has blessed you forever.
NRSVCE.3:
Gird your sword on your thigh, O mighty one,
in your glory and majesty.
NRSVCE.4:
In your majesty ride on victoriously
for the cause of truth and to defend the right;
let your right hand teach you dread deeds.
NRSVCE.5:
Your arrows are sharp
in the heart of the king’s enemies;
the peoples fall under you.
NRSVCE.6:
Your throne, O God, endures forever and ever.
Your royal scepter is a scepter of equity;
NRSVCE.7:
you love righteousness and hate wickedness.
Therefore God, your God, has anointed you
with the oil of gladness beyond your companions;
NRSVCE.8:
your robes are all fragrant with myrrh and aloes and cassia.
From ivory palaces stringed instruments make you glad;
NRSVCE.9:
daughters of kings are among your ladies of honor;
at your right hand stands the queen in gold of Ophir.
NRSVCE.10:
Hear, O daughter, consider and incline your ear;
forget your people and your father’s house,
NRSVCE.11:
and the king will desire your beauty.
Since he is your lord, bow to him;
NRSVCE.12:
the people of Tyre will seek your favor with gifts,
the richest of the people
NRSVCE.13:
with all kinds of wealth.
The princess is decked in her chamber with gold-woven robes;
NRSVCE.14:
in many-colored robes she is led to the king;
behind her the virgins, her companions, follow.
NRSVCE.15:
With joy and gladness they are led along
as they enter the palace of the king.
NRSVCE.16:
In the place of ancestors you, O king, shall have sons;
you will make them princes in all the earth.
NRSVCE.17:
I will cause your name to be celebrated in all generations;
therefore the peoples will praise you forever and ever.

NET.1: My heart is stirred by a beautiful song. I say, “I have composed this special song for the king; my tongue is as skilled as the stylus of an experienced scribe.”
NET.2: You are the most handsome of all men! You speak in an impressive and fitting manner! For this reason God grants you continual blessings.
NET.3: Strap your sword to your thigh, O warrior! Appear in your majestic splendor!
NET.4: Appear in your majesty and be victorious! Ride forth for the sake of what is right, on behalf of justice! Then your right hand will accomplish mighty acts!
NET.5: Your arrows are sharp and penetrate the hearts of the king’s enemies. Nations fall at your feet.
NET.6: Your throne, O God, is permanent. The scepter of your kingdom is a scepter of justice.
NET.7: You love justice and hate evil. For this reason God, your God has anointed you with the oil of joy, elevating you above your companions.
NET.8: All your garments are perfumed with myrrh, aloes, and cassia. From the luxurious palaces comes the music of stringed instruments that makes you happy.
NET.9: Princesses are among your honored guests, your bride stands at your right hand, wearing jewelry made with gold from Ophir.
NET.10: Listen, O princess! Observe and pay attention! Forget your homeland and your family!
NET.11: Then the king will be attracted by your beauty. After all, he is your master! Submit to him!
NET.12: Rich people from Tyre will seek your favor by bringing a gift.
NET.13: The princess looks absolutely magnificent, decked out in pearls and clothed in a brocade trimmed with gold.
NET.14: In embroidered robes she is escorted to the king. Her attendants, the maidens of honor who follow her, are led before you.
NET.15: They are bubbling with joy as they walk in procession and enter the royal palace.
NET.16: Your sons will carry on the dynasty of your ancestors; you will make them princes throughout the land.
NET.17: I will proclaim your greatness through the coming years, then the nations will praise you forever.
